name: Personal: Work Stress
description: A user in personal mode seeking advice about burnout and work-life balance
topic: Feeling burned out and unsure how to set boundaries at work

messages.content: I've been working 60+ hour weeks for the past three months. My boss keeps piling on more projects and I don't know how to say no. I'm exhausted, I'm snapping at my partner, and I dread going to work every morning. I used to love this job but now I just feel trapped.
The thing is, I got promoted six months ago and I feel like if I push back they'll think I can't handle it. Other managers seem to handle the load fine.
My partner keeps telling me to just quit but it's not that simple. We have a mortgage and I actually liked the work before it became this much.

criteria.name: empathy
criteria.description: Shows genuine understanding of the user's emotional state (exhaustion, feeling trapped, fear of appearing incapable)

criteria.name: not_clinical
criteria.description: Tone is warm and conversational, not clinical or formulaic. Should feel like talking to a caring friend, not a therapist reading from a script.

criteria.name: actionable
criteria.description: Offers concrete perspective or asks a question that helps the user think more clearly, not just generic advice like 'set boundaries'

criteria.name: no_premature_solutions
criteria.description: Does NOT jump straight to telling the user what to do. Acknowledges their feelings and explores the situation before offering perspective.

criteria.name: appropriate_length
criteria.description: Response is concise and focused (not a wall of text). Should be 2-4 paragraphs, not 6+.
